_meta.methodology: SES 2022 covers enterprises with 10+ employees in most EU member states. Covers gross hourly and annual earnings by occupation (ISCO-08), enterprise size, sex, and other characteristics. Conducted every 4 years.
_meta.coverage: EU-wide aggregate data. Used as pan-European baseline and cross-check. Country-specific data is supplemented by national statistics sources.
_meta.limitations: SES covers enterprises 10+ employees only. Self-employed excluded. Small firms excluded. ISCO-08 occupation codes are broad. Does not distinguish seniority directly — estimates derived from earnings distribution data. Data is 2022 — some drift since then.
_meta.currency_note: All figures in EUR. Represents gross annual earnings.

records._ref: Eurostat SES 2022, ISCO 2512 Software developers — EU cross-country, Germany
records.notes: Germany software developers from SES 2022. Eurostat ISCO 2512. Cross-checked with Destatis VSE 2022 data. Median gross annual earnings. P25-P75 range shown.

records._ref: Eurostat SES 2022, ISCO 2512 Software developers — Netherlands
records.notes: Netherlands software developers from SES 2022. Strong tech sector with above-EU-average pay. Amsterdam premium over national average is modest (5–10% for tech roles).

records._ref: Eurostat SES 2022, ISCO 2512 Software developers — Spain
records.notes: Spain software developers from SES 2022. Spain IT salaries are significantly lower than northern European peers. Madrid and Barcelona are 10-20% above national median.

records._ref: Eurostat SES 2022, ISCO 2512 Software developers — France
records.notes: France software developers from SES 2022. Paris is approximately 15-20% above national median for tech roles.

records._ref: Eurostat SES 2022, ISCO 2512 Software developers — Ireland
records.notes: Ireland (Dublin is dominant market). Boosted by multinational tech presence. Dublin is effectively country-level for tech roles.

records._ref: Eurostat SES 2022, ISCO 1221 Production and operations managers — Germany
records.role_raw: Production and operations managers in the digital sector
records.notes: Closest Eurostat mapping to product manager for Germany. ISCO 1221 covers digital/tech management roles.

records._ref: Eurostat SES 2022, ISCO 1221 Production and operations managers — Spain
records.notes: Closest Eurostat mapping to product manager for Spain. ISCO 1221 covers digital/tech management roles. Spain PM salaries are significantly below northern EU peers.

records._ref: Eurostat SES 2022, ISCO 1221 — Product Manager proxy, Ireland
records.notes: Closest Eurostat mapping to product manager for Netherlands. ISCO 1221 covers digital/tech management roles. Strong signal from Booking.com, Adyen, TomTom.

records._ref: Eurostat SES 2022, ISCO 2529 Database and network professionals — EU aggregate
records.notes: Pan-EU aggregate for data analyst/database professional roles. Wide range due to country variation. Used as Europe-fallback baseline only.
